title: USDA Single-Layer Spec Coverage Ledger

normative_source.rule: The pinned specification is the source of truth for USD behavior and USDA grammar. Contracts, handles, skills, and goldens decompose that specification and add library-quality constraints; they must not relax or replace the specification.

contract_role.grammar_contracts: State which pinned spec productions are accepted or rejected in each parser context.
contract_role.mapping_contracts: Map valid productions to document-model fields and target-native domain values.
contract_role.handle_contracts: Define stable conformance surfaces used by generated targets and adapters.
contract_role.quality_contracts: Forbid toy implementations such as JSON-as-storage, parser-local paths, raw string field identity, or non-native specialized values.
contract_role.performance_contracts: Attach workflow-specific targets and operation flatness requirements where the specification is intentionally abstract.

coverage_rules: Every production in this scope must be implemented, deferred with an explicit reason, or marked out of scope for this subgraph.
A production marked implemented must have at least one positive golden, and ambiguous/context-sensitive productions must have negative goldens.
Parsing must be grammar-first. Implementations match a valid USDA production in the current context before selecting document-model storage.
Document-model field names do not make a token stream legal. Reserved USDA keywords are accepted only by productions that name those keywords.
Storage mappings and quality floors apply after successful grammar recognition.

status_values.implemented: In scope for this subgraph and covered by contracts/goldens.
status_values.deferred: Specified but intentionally not implemented in this subgraph; the reason must name the owning future capability.
status_values.out_of_scope: Belongs to a different format or higher-level USD subsystem.

grammar_obligations: Identifier excludes every Keyword.
NamespacedName is based on BaseIdentifier and may contain keyword spellings where the grammar permits names.
Metadata keys using KeyValueMetadata/ListOpMetadata consume Identifier, not BaseIdentifier.
quality_floor: Identifier validation is owned by usd-identifiers-and-names/usda-lexical-format, not reimplemented ad hoc in the spec parser.

grammar_obligations: Layer metadata accepts SharedMetadata, DocMetadata, ListOpMetadata, SublayerMetadata, and RelocatesMetadata.
Prim metadata accepts SharedMetadata, ListOpMetadata, KindMetadata, PayloadMetadata, InheritsMetadata, SpecializesMetadata, ReferencesMetadata, VariantsMetadata, and VariantSetsMetadata.
Attribute metadata accepts SharedMetadata and ListOpMetadata only.
Relationship metadata accepts SharedMetadata and ListOpMetadata only.
Keyword metadata productions must be selected by context before storage mapping.
quality_floor: Metadata parsing may share helpers, but helpers must be parameterized by the grammar production family and must reject keywords that are not legal in the current context.
Known document-model fields must not bypass grammar validation.

grammar_obligations: ReferenceList and PayloadList accept None, a single item, an empty bracketed list, or a bracketed list of items.
Reference/Payload items may be asset refs, path refs, or asset+path pairs with permitted layer offset parameters.
References allow customData parameters; payloads do not.
A missing listOp token authors the explicit listOp subfield.
quality_floor: Composition arc records are stored inertly and natively; they are not dictionaries, JSON objects, bare asset strings, or bare path strings.
No composition, asset loading, or value resolution is performed.

grammar_obligations: Attribute declarations produce attribute specs with custom, variability, typeName, and property path.
Special attribute forms .timeSamples, .connect, and .spline are property productions, not generic metadata.
ListOpConnect shares a leading listOp token with ListOpRelationship and must be dispatched by following tokens.
quality_floor: TimeSamples and Spline are native domain values.
Connect targets are authored listOp<ObjectPath> values and are not resolved.

grammar_obligations: Relationships accept rel/custom rel, target assignment to None, single path refs, or path-ref lists.
ListOpRelationship authors targetPaths subfields.
Relationship metadata does not accept prim-only metadata keywords.
quality_floor: Target paths are authored listOp<ObjectPath> values and are not resolved.

grammar_obligations: subLayers accepts a list of asset refs with optional layer offset parameters.
relocates accepts path-ref to path-ref pairs and path-ref to None deletes.
quality_floor: subLayers/subLayerOffsets and Relocates are native domain values.
Parsing sublayers does not load or resolve referenced layers.

spec_refs: document_data_model/README.md:105 Variant and Variant Set Specs
grammar_obligations: variantSet statements create variant set specs and variant specs.
variant contents parse with prim-like contents but do not perform variant selection.
quality_floor: Variant specs and children are stored in the document model; stage population is not performed.

spec_refs: composition sections outside the current single-layer open scope
productions.deferred_reason: This subgraph opens and stores one layer. It intentionally does not compose sublayers, references, payloads, inherits, specializes, variants, relocates, or resolved values.
This scope covers USDA text only. USDC and USDZ should be separate format skills that consume the same document-model contracts.
